steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Reduce by full rotations
steps.content: Cosine repeats every $2\pi$. Since $$\frac{565\pi}{6}=\frac{564\pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{6}=47(2\pi)+\frac{\pi}{6},$$ the angle is coterminal with $\frac{\pi}{6}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ is correct. $\frac{1}{2}$ is $\sin(\pi/6)$, $1$ is $\cos(0)$, and $\sqrt{3}$ is too large for a cosine value. The reference angle after reducing is exactly $\pi/6$.
